Title: The biosynthesis, degradation, and function of cell wall β-xylosylated xyloglucan mirrors that of arabinoxyloglucan

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β1,2-xylosylation branch of xyloglucan side-chain decoration
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: xyloglucan XXXG repeat acceptor; likely UDP-xylose donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: XUXG (also likely XUGG, XUUG, XUFG in Arabidopsis overexpression lines)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a β1,2-linked xylosyl residue onto the second α-xylosyl sidechain of the XXXG xyloglucan repeat, generating XUXG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber component; functionally replaces fucogalactoxyloglucan/arabinoxyloglucan in plant growth; may nourish beneficial gut bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: XXT2 promoter (constitutive/xyloglucan-biosynthetic tissue-associated); CESA3 promoter (strong primary-cell-wall-specific)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mRNA detected most successfully in roots and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transgenically expressed in Arabidopsis leaves/primary wall tissues
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus (strongly inferred for GT47 xyloglucan glycosyltransferase)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces novel β-xylosylated xyloglucan into Arabidopsis and fully rescues growth defects of xyloglucan galactosyltransferase mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in Arabidopsis mutants; phenotypic complementation; PACE; MALDI-TOF MS; exoglycosidase digestion with CgGH3/CjAbf51; SEC purification; NMR; monosaccharide analysis; CID-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth at 21°C under 16 h:8 h photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: phylogenetic/genome sequence mining from published genomic datasets; no accession-specific omics dataset reported for this gene in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression may alter α-xylosylation pattern and generate atypical XUGG/XUG products; no clear growth penalty reported in complementation lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to engineer β-xylosylated xyloglucan fiber structures and restore wall-function defects; useful for tailoring dietary fiber composition and potentially modulating gut microbiome utilization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A dedicated transferase rather than galactosyltransferase side activity; activity is strongly specific for the second xylosyl residue in XXXG; presence in coffee suggests β-xylosylated xyloglucan may be more widespread than previously recognized
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcXBT1 from Coffea canephora is a dedicated xyloglucan β-xylosyltransferase in GT47-AIIIc2. 2. When expressed in Arabidopsis, it generates XUXG-centered β-xylosylated xyloglucan structures. 3. CcXBT1 fully rescues mur3-3 and xlt2 mur3-1 growth defects, showing β-xylosylated xyloglucan can functionally replace missing galactosylated sidechains.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: arabinofuranosylation branch of xyloglucan side-chain decoration
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: xyloglucan XXXG repeat acceptor; likely UDP-Araf donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an arabinofuranosyl residue to xyloglucan with the same apparent product specificity as SlXST1 when expressed in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall structural polysaccharide/dietary fiber; can partially replace missing galactosylated xyloglucan sidechains for plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transgenically expressed in Arabidopsis leaves/primary wall tissues
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus (strongly inferred for GT47 xyloglucan glycosyltransferase)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Weakly introduces arabinofuranosylated xyloglucan and partially complements Arabidopsis xyloglucan galactosyltransferase mutant growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in Arabidopsis mutants; phenotypic complementation; PACE; MALDI-TOF MS; exoglycosidase digestion with CjAbf51
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth at 21°C under 16 h:8 h photoperiod
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: phylogenetic/genome sequence mining from published genomic datasets; no accession-specific omics dataset reported for this gene in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activity is weak in Arabidopsis and complementation is incomplete/variable between lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for engineering arabinoxyloglucan-like fiber structures, though low activity may require stronger paralogues or stacking
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The synthesized construct had a deletion of repetitive/putatively disordered stem residues 167–193 to maximize expression; authors suggest it may be redundant to more active paralogues in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmXST1 is a putative xyloglucan arabinofuranosyltransferase from cranberry. 2. In Arabidopsis it generates a product co-migrating with SlXST1-derived XXSG and this product is removed by α-arabinofuranosidase. 3. VmXST1 shows weak activity and only partial complementation of xyloglucan mutant phenotypes.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: gut microbial debranching of arabinofuranosylated and β-xylosylated xyloglucan sidechains
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: X-arabinofuranosylated xyloglucan oligosaccharides (e.g. XXSG, XLSG, XSSG); β-xylosylated xyloglucan oligosaccharides (e.g. XUXG, XUFG); pNP-α-Araf; pNP-β-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses terminal α-arabinofuranosyl and β1,2-xylosyl decorations from xyloglucan oligosaccharides, converting decorated substrates such as XXSG and XUXG to XXXG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber substrates for gut microbiota metabolism leading to beneficial short-chain fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plant primary cell wall; dietary plant material in gut
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: in vitro enzyme assays on plant and synthetic substrates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: encoded in the xyloglucan utilization locus (XyGUL)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently degrades both arabinofuranosylated and β-xylosylated xyloglucan oligosaccharides, indicating a promiscuous dual function in gut fiber utilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant protein purification; kinetic assays on pNP glycosides; pH profiling; Michaelis-Menten analysis; digestion of transgenic and natural xyloglucan oligosaccharides followed by PACE and MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major debranching enzyme for these substrates in the studied system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: enzyme assays at defined pH; optimum near pH 7.5 in 50 mM HEPES
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Fails to remove an unidentified pentosyl decoration from blueberry xyloglucan, indicating substrate limitations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme for analytical deconstruction of complex xyloglucan fibers and for designing/preassessing dietary fibers that support beneficial gut microbiota
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude BoGH43A is probably the major enzyme responsible for processing both arabinoxyloglucan and β-xylosylated xyloglucan in B. ovatus; more active than BoGH43B on native substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BoGH43A is a dual-function, promiscuous α-arabinofuranosidase/β-xylosidase. 2. It efficiently hydrolyses both XXSG-type and XUXG/XUFG-type xyloglucan oligosaccharides from transgenic and natural sources. 3. Its promiscuity supports a gut microbial pathway for degradation of β-xylosylated xyloglucan.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: gut microbial debranching of arabinofuranosylated xyloglucan sidechains
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: pNP-α-Araf; pNP-β-Xyl; weak activity on XXSG
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses synthetic α-arabinofuranoside and β-xyloside substrates weakly; shows only minor activity on XXSG and no detectable activity on XUXG under the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: dietary fiber substrates for gut microbiota metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: plant primary cell wall; dietary plant material in gut
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: in vitro enzyme assays on plant and synthetic substrates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: encoded in the xyloglucan utilization locus (XyGUL) adjacent to BoGH43A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Displays much lower catalytic efficiency than BoGH43A and little to no activity on native β-xylosylated xyloglucan oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant protein purification; kinetic assays on pNP glycosides; pH profiling; Michaelis-Menten analysis; digestion of xyloglucan oligosaccharides followed by PACE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: enzyme assays at defined pH; optimum near pH 7.5 in 50 mM HEPES
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low activity on native substrates limits utility for β-xylosylated xyloglucan degradation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily relevant as a comparative enzyme showing that not all GH43 homologs are equally useful for xyloglucan debranching
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promiscuity on pNP sugars does not translate to effective activity on native XUXG substrate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BoGH43B hydrolyses pNP-α-Araf and pNP-β-Xyl but is much weaker than BoGH43A. 2. It shows only slight activity on XXSG and no detectable activity on XUXG. 3. The paper suggests BoGH43B is not the main enzyme for β-xylosylated xyloglucan processing under tested conditions.
